claims.text: Elite performing teams deploy on demand, multiple times per day.

evidence_spans.text: Elite performing teams deploy on demand, multiple times per day.

claims.text: A deployment pipeline automates the steps of building, testing, and deploying the application.

evidence_spans.text: A deployment pipeline automates the steps of building, testing, and deploying the application.

claims.text: Developers should commit small changes to trunk at least once a day to avoid long-lived divergent branches.

evidence_spans.text: Developers commit small changes to trunk at least once a day to avoid long-lived divergent branches.

claims.text: The incident commander is the single person responsible for coordinating the response during an active incident. The incident commander delegates tasks, tracks status, and makes decisions, but does not necessarily fix the problem directly.

evidence_spans.text: The incident commander is the single person responsible for coordinating the response during an active incident. The incident commander delegates tasks, tracks status, and makes decisions, but does not necessarily fix the problem directly.

claims.text: Teams should migrate from the solo method to the single-team method when a second contributor joins the project.

evidence_spans.text: When a second contributor joins the project, teams should migrate from the solo method to the single-team method.

claims.text: The assured method provides bidirectional traceability between requirements, design, and tests using positional namespace identifiers.

evidence_spans.text: The assured method provides bidirectional traceability between requirements, design, and tests using positional namespace identifiers.

claims.text: The technical debt metaphor was coined by Ward Cunningham.

evidence_spans.text: The metaphor was coined by Ward Cunningham.

claims.text: A changelog records the notable changes since the previous release. A release should be accompanied by a changelog.

evidence_spans.text: A release should be accompanied by a changelog that records the notable changes since the previous release.

claims.text: The observability.md page describes metrics, logs, and traces. It covers the three pillars of observability (metrics, logs, traces), service level objectives, and error budgets.

evidence_spans.text: The three pillars of observability are metrics, logs, and traces.

claims.text: The ci-cd.md page explains the deployment pipeline. It describes how a deployment pipeline automates the steps of building, testing, and deploying the application as part of continuous delivery.

evidence_spans.text: A deployment pipeline automates the steps of building, testing, and deploying the application.
